feedback_items.title: Implement tap feedback for interactive elements
feedback_items.why_it_matters: Users need to know if their taps are registering. Lack of feedback leads to uncertainty and frustration, especially with network delays.
what_to_do: Add a subtle visual cue (e.g., ripple effect, slight color change, animation) upon tapping any interactive element like Action Cards, Category Cards, Hamburger Menu, and the Money Bag icon.
Ensure the feedback is immediate and clearly visible to the user.
feedback_items.wireframe_changes: Add a note to the wireframe showing visual feedback states for all touch targets.  Specify the type of feedback (color change, animation, etc.).

feedback_items.title: Introduce loading indicators for content fetching
feedback_items.why_it_matters: Users need to understand when content is loading. Without indicators, the app can appear frozen, leading to a poor user experience.
what_to_do: Implement skeleton loaders or progress indicators when new category content or quiz data is being fetched.
Use a determinate progress bar when the loading progress can be accurately tracked, and an indeterminate progress bar when it cannot.
Ensure the loading indicators are visually consistent with the app's overall design.
feedback_items.wireframe_changes: Add skeleton loading states or progress bar visuals to the wireframes for Category Cards and other areas where data is fetched dynamically.

feedback_items.title: Implement confirmation dialog for 'Redeem' action
feedback_items.why_it_matters: Redeeming virtual currency is potentially irreversible. A confirmation dialog prevents accidental actions and user dissatisfaction.
what_to_do: Add a confirmation dialog before processing the 'Redeem' action.
The dialog should clearly state the action being performed and the consequences (e.g., 'Are you sure you want to redeem [amount] for [item]?').
feedback_items.wireframe_changes: Add a wireframe for the 'Redeem' confirmation dialog, showing the message and confirmation/cancel buttons.

feedback_items.title: Clearly disable 'QUIZ' button when unavailable
feedback_items.why_it_matters: Users need to understand when a quiz is not available. Ambiguous button states lead to confusion and frustration.
what_to_do: If a quiz is not available for a category, the 'QUIZ' button should be clearly disabled (e.g., grayed out, with a different appearance).
Add a tooltip or short explanation when hovering over or tapping the disabled button (e.g., 'Quiz unavailable').
feedback_items.wireframe_changes: Add a wireframe state for the Category Card showing the disabled 'QUIZ' button and tooltip.

feedback_items.title: Implement user-friendly error messages
feedback_items.why_it_matters: Clear error messages help users understand and recover from problems.
what_to_do: Implement user-friendly error messages that clearly state what went wrong, why it happened (if possible), and suggest actionable steps for recovery (e.g., 'Network error. Please check your connection and try again.').
Ensure error messages are displayed prominently and are visually distinct from other UI elements.
feedback_items.wireframe_changes: Add wireframe examples of common error message displays (e.g., network error, redemption failure) within the application context.

feedback_items.title: Implement advanced interaction patterns (long-press)
feedback_items.why_it_matters: Power users benefit from shortcuts. Long-press can provide quicker access to frequently used features.
what_to_do: Consider adding long-press functionality to Category Cards (e.g., long-press for 'Play Random Quiz').
Consider swipe gestures for navigation if this is a multi-screen application.
Make sure these features don't interfere with the core functionality of single-tap interactions.
feedback_items.wireframe_changes: Add a wireframe showing the UI response to a long-press action on a Category Card (e.g., displaying a context menu with 'Play Random Quiz').

feedback_items.title: Implement a 'Help' or 'FAQ' section
feedback_items.why_it_matters: Help documentation assists users, especially new ones or those encountering problems.
what_to_do: Ensure a 'Help' or 'FAQ' section is easily accessible, likely via the Hamburger Menu.
Populate the section with frequently asked questions and troubleshooting guides.
feedback_items.wireframe_changes: Create wireframes for the 'Help' section, including the layout and example content.

quick_wins.change: Improve contrast on 'Beginner' subtitle on gradient backgrounds.
Use consistent styling for all buttons.

implementation_order: Implement tap feedback for interactive elements
Introduce loading indicators for content fetching
